data.claim: Random Forest substantially outperforms logistic regression for predicting civil war onset on the Muchlinski 2016 panel dataset.

data.name: Imputation-before-split leakage
data.reason: KNN imputation fits on the full dataframe before train/test split.

data.name: Target column included in imputation matrix
data.reason: If the target column is in the dataframe at impute time, KNN uses y as a neighbor-matching feature.

data.name: Class-weight or sampling mismatch vs the paper
data.reason: Muchlinski uses class-balancing; RF and LR may be tuned asymmetrically.

data.name: Temporal structure ignored in split
data.reason: Country-year panel with random split could inflate both models if years leak.

data.name: Read
data.output:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.impute import KNNImputer
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
...
def prepare(df, test_size=0.25):
    df = df.drop(columns=ID_COLS).copy()
    imputer = KNNImputer(n_neighbors=5)
    df_imputed = pd.DataFrame(imputer.fit_transform(df), columns=df.columns, index=df.index)
    y = df_imputed[TARGET].round().astype(int)
    X = df_imputed.drop(columns=[TARGET])
    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, stratify=y)
    return X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test

data.description: Inspect prepare_data.py for imputer fit order relative to train_test_split.

data.result: prepare_data.py calls imputer.fit_transform(df) on the full dataframe before train_test_split. Confirmed.

data.description: Check whether TARGET is in df at imputation time.

data.result: Line 24 drops only ID_COLS before imputation; TARGET stays in df. KNN uses target as a feature. Second confirmed leak.

data.summary: Compound imputation leakage: imputation-before-split AND target in imputation matrix. Both must be fixed.

input.old_string:
df = df.drop(columns=ID_COLS).copy()

    # Impute missing values across the full dataframe.
    imputer = KNNImputer(n_neighbors=5)
    df_imputed = pd.DataFrame(
        imputer.fit_transform(df),
        columns=df.columns,
        index=df.index,
    )

    y = df_imputed[TARGET].round().astype(int)
    X = df_imputed.drop(columns=[TARGET])

    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
        X,
        y,
        test_size=test_size,
        random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
        stratify=y,
    )
input.new_string:
y = df[TARGET]
    X = df.drop(columns=[TARGET, *ID_COLS])

    X_train_raw, X_test_raw, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
        X, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, stratify=y,
    )
    imputer = KNNImputer(n_neighbors=5)
    X_train = pd.DataFrame(imputer.fit_transform(X_train_raw), columns=X.columns, index=X_train_raw.index)
    X_test  = pd.DataFrame(imputer.transform(X_test_raw),     columns=X.columns, index=X_test_raw.index)

data.diff_summary: Drop target + ID up front; split first; fit KNNImputer on X_train only; transform X_test with fitted imputer.

data.name: Bash

data.markdown: The repo reports RF=0.9562, LR=0.8091 on a 25% hold-out; the implied research claim is RF substantially outperforms LR.
prepare_data.py (lines 24–28) fits KNN imputation on the full dataframe including the target column. Both leakage conditions confirmed by direct inspection.
Compound leakage via imputation. (1) Imputation before split. (2) Target in imputation matrix.
Drop target and ID columns up front; split first; fit KNNImputer on X_train only and apply transform to X_test.
Depends on random seed; CV over 5 seeds would tighten the LR estimate. Patient/country-level leakage was not investigated and remains a follow-up.

input.command: cd /tmp/muchlinski-demo && git checkout -b fix/reproducibility-$(date +%s) && git add -A && git commit -m 'Fix imputation leakage' && git push -u origin HEAD
